subject: Mathematics
q: If the range of the function $f(x)=\frac{5-x}{x^2-3 x+2}$, $x \neq 1,2$, is $(-\infty, \alpha] \cup[\beta, \infty)$, then $\alpha^2+\beta^2$ is equal to :
question: If the range of the function $f(x)=\frac{5-x}{x^2-3 x+2}$, $x \neq 1,2$, is $(-\infty, \alpha] \cup[\beta, \infty)$, then $\alpha^2+\beta^2$ is equal to :
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& y=\frac{5-x}{x^2-3 x+2} \\ & y x^2-3 x y+2 y+x-5=0 \\ & y z^2+(-3 y+1) x+(2 y-5)=0
\end{aligned}$
Case I : If $y=0$ (Accepted)
$\Rightarrow x=5$
Case II : If $y \neq 0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{D} \geq 0 \\ & (-3 y+1)^2-4(y)(2 y-5) \geq 0 \\ & 9 y^2+1-6 y-8 y^2+20 y \geq 0 \\ & \mathrm{y}^2+14 y+1 \geq 0 \\ & (y+7)^2-48 \geq 0 \\ & |y+7| \geq 4 \sqrt{3} \\ & \Rightarrow y+7 \geq 4 \sqrt{3} \text { or } \mathrm{y}+7 \leq-4 \sqrt{3} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{y} \geq 4 \sqrt{3}-7 \text { or } \mathrm{y} \leq-4 \sqrt{3}-7
\end{aligned}$
From Case I and Case II
$\mathrm{y} \in(-\infty,-4 \sqrt{3}-7] \cup[4 \sqrt{3}-7, \infty)$
So $\alpha=-4 \sqrt{3}-7$
$\beta=4 \sqrt{3}-7$
$\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow a^2+b^2 & =(-4 \sqrt{3}-7)^2+(4 \sqrt{3}-7)^2 \\ & =2(48+49) \\ & =194
\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $f:(-1,1) \rightarrow B$, be a function defined by $f(x)=\tan ^{-1} \frac{2 x}{1-x^2}$, then $f$ is both one-one and onto when $B$ is the interval
question: Let $f:(-1,1) \rightarrow B$, be a function defined by $f(x)=\tan ^{-1} \frac{2 x}{1-x^2}$, then $f$ is both one-one and onto when $B$ is the interval
solution:
Given $f(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right)$ for $x \in(-1,1)$
clearly range of $f(x)=\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$.
$\therefore$ co-domain of function $=B=\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$.

subject: Mathematics
q: The minimum value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></math> is :
question: The minimum value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></math> is :
solution:
Using A.M≥G.M.

2sinx+2cosx2≥2sinx·2cosx

⇒2sinx+2cosx≥21+sinx+cosx2

Now    -2≤sinx+cosx≤2

So minimum value of 

2sinx+2cosx=21+-22=21-12

subject: Mathematics
question: If $g(x)=3 x^{2}+2 x-3, f(0)=-3$ and $4 g(f(x))=3 x^{2}-32 x+72$, then $f(g(2))$ is equal to:
solution:
Let $f(x) = ax + b$. Then $4g(f(x)) = 12(ax+b)^2 + 8(ax+b) - 12$.
Comparing with $3x^2 - 32x + 72$:
$x^2$: $12a^2 = 3 \Rightarrow a = \pm\frac{1}{2}$
Constant: $12b^2 + 8b - 12 = 72 \Rightarrow 3b^2 + 2b - 21 = 0 \Rightarrow b = \frac{7}{3}$ or $b = -3$. Since $f(0) = -3$, $b = -3$.
$x$: $8a(3(-3)+1) = -32 \Rightarrow -8a = -4 \Rightarrow a = \frac{1}{2}$.
So $f(x) = \frac{x}{2} - 3$.
$g(2) = 3(4) + 4 - 3 = 13$.
$f(g(2)) = f(13) = \frac{13}{2} - 3 = \frac{7}{2}$.

f′x=-2x-12<0

Clearly, given function is one-one or injective.

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced></math> is ______.
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> be two real polynomials of degree <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> respectively. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced></math> is ______.
solution:
Given, fgx=8x2-2xand  gfx=4x2+6x+1

Let fx=cx2+dx+e and gx=ax+b

So, fgx=cax+b2+dax+b+e=8x2-2x

and gfx=acx2+dx+e+b=4x2+6x+1

On comparing both side we get, a=2, b=-1, c=2, d=3, e=1

So, gx=2x-1⇒g2=3

& fx=2x2+3x+1

f2=8+6+1=15.

So, f2+g2=18

solution:
afx+αf1x=bx+βx …1

replace x by 1x

af1x+αfx=bx+βx …2

1+2

a+αfx+a+αf1x=xb+β+b+β1x

fx+f1xx+1x=b+βa+α=21=2

only '2' in range →1 function.

q: If fx=4x+36x-4, x≠23 and (fof) (x)=g(x), where g:R-23→R-23, then (gogog) (4) is equal to
question: If fx=4x+36x-4, x≠23 and (fof) (x)=g(x), where g:R-23→R-23, then (gogog) (4) is equal to
solution:
Given: fx=4x+36x-4

⇒fofx=44x+36x-4+364x+36x-4-4

⇒fofx=16x+12+18x-1224x+18-24x+16

⇒fofx=34x34

⇒fofx=x

⇒gx=x

⇒gogx=x

⇒gogogx=x

⇒gogog4=4

subject: Mathematics
q: Let D be the domain of the function fx=sin-1log3x6+2log3x-5x. If the range of the function g:D→ℝ defined by gx=x-x, (x is the greatest integer function), is α, β, then α2+5β is equal to
question: Let D be the domain of the function fx=sin-1log3x6+2log3x-5x. If the range of the function g:D→ℝ defined by gx=x-x, (x is the greatest integer function), is α, β, then α2+5β is equal to
solution:
We have been given

fx=sin-1log3x6+2log3x-5x

We know that the domain of sin-1x is -1≤x≤1.

⇒log3x6+2log3x-5x∈-1  1-1, 3x>0-2, 3x≠1-3, 6+2log3x-5x-4>0

⇒ From 2, x>0, from 3, x≠13

From 4, 6+2log3x<0    ∵ x>0

⇒log3x<-3

⇒x<3-3

⇒x<127 

From 2, 3, 4, 0<x<127     ...5

From 1, -1≤log3x6+2log3x-5x≤1

∵ 3x∈0, 19

⇒13x≥6+2log3x-5x≥3x

⇒-53≤6+2log3x≤-15x2

⇒-236≤log3x≤-15x2-62

⇒3-236≤x≤3-15x2-62

⇒13236≤x≤1315x2+62 

⇒3-236≤x<1315x2+62  ......6, here 3-236 is very small positive quantity,

From 5 and 6, 3-236<x<127, x=0

⇒gx=x

⇒ Range gx is domain of fx

⇒gx∈3-236,127≡α,β.

⇒α2+5β=3-2362+5127=3-2362+135≈135

Note: This question was bonus in Jee Main April 2023 session.

question: For <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>-</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>x</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> be three given functions. If a function, <math><mi>J</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> satisfies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>o</mi><mi>J</mi><mi>o</mi><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> then <math><mi>J</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given f2Jf1x=f3x

⇒f2J1x=11-x

⇒1-J1x=11-x

⇒J1x=1-11-x=-x1-x

⇒Jx=-1x1-1x

=-1x-1

=11-x

∴ Jx=f3x

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $e$ be the base of natural logarithm and let $f: \{1, 2, 3, 4\} \rightarrow \{1, e, e^2, e^3\}$ and $g: \{1, e, e^2, e^3\} \rightarrow \left\{1, \dfrac{1}{2}, \dfrac{1}{3}, \dfrac{1}{4}\right\}$ be two bijective functions such that $f$ is strictly decreasing and $g$ is strictly increasing. If $\phi(x) = \left[f^{-1}\left\{g^{-1}\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)\right\}\right]^x$, then the area of the region $R = \{(x, y): x^2 \leq y \leq \phi(x), 0 \leq x \leq 1\}$ is:
question: Let $e$ be the base of natural logarithm and let $f: \{1, 2, 3, 4\} \rightarrow \{1, e, e^2, e^3\}$ and $g: \{1, e, e^2, e^3\} \rightarrow \left\{1, \dfrac{1}{2}, \dfrac{1}{3}, \dfrac{1}{4}\right\}$ be two bijective functions such that $f$ is strictly decreasing and $g$ is strictly increasing. If $\phi(x) = \left[f^{-1}\left\{g^{-1}\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)\right\}\right]^x$, then the area of the region $R = \{(x, y): x^2 \leq y \leq \phi(x), 0 \leq x \leq 1\}$ is:
solution:
Given $f: \{1, 2, 3, 4\} \rightarrow \{1, e, e^2, e^3\}$ is a strictly decreasing bijective function.

Arranging the domain and codomain in increasing order, we get $f(1) = e^3$, $f(2) = e^2$, $f(3) = e$, and $f(4) = 1$.

Given $g: \{1, e, e^2, e^3\} \rightarrow \left\{1, \dfrac{1}{2}, \dfrac{1}{3}, \dfrac{1}{4}\right\}$ is a strictly increasing bijective function.

Arranging the domain and codomain in increasing order, we get $g(1) = \dfrac{1}{4}$, $g(e) = \dfrac{1}{3}$, $g(e^2) = \dfrac{1}{2}$, and $g(e^3) = 1$.

Evaluating $\phi(x) = \left[f^{-1}\left\{g^{-1}\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)\right\}\right]^x$:

From the mapping of $g$, $g^{-1}\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right) = e^2$.

From the mapping of $f$, $f^{-1}(e^2) = 2$.

Substituting these values, we get $\phi(x) = 2^x$.

The region $R$ is given by $x^2 \leq y \leq 2^x$ for $0 \leq x \leq 1$.

The area of the region $R$ is:

$\int_{0}^{1} (2^x - x^2) dx = \left[ \dfrac{2^x}{\log_e(2)} - \dfrac{x^3}{3} \right]_{0}^{1}$

$= \left( \dfrac{2}{\log_e(2)} - \dfrac{1}{3} \right) - \left( \dfrac{1}{\log_e(2)} - 0 \right)$

$= \dfrac{1}{\log_e(2)} - \dfrac{1}{3}$

$= \dfrac{3 - \log_e(2)}{3\log_e(2)}$

Answer: $\dfrac{3 - \log_e(2)}{3\log_e(2)}$

options: not injective but it is surjective
solution:
$f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow(0, \infty)$

$f(x)=\left|1-\frac{1}{x}\right|$ is not a function

$\because f(1)=0$ and $1 \in$ domain but $0 \notin$ co-domain

Hence, $f(x)$ is not a function.

q:
Let $A$ and $B$ be non empty sets in $R$ and $f: A \rightarrow B$ is a bijective function.
Statement 1: $\mathrm{f}$ is an onto function.
Statement 2: There exists a function $g: B \rightarrow A$ such that fog $=I_B$.
question:
Let $A$ and $B$ be non empty sets in $R$ and $f: A \rightarrow B$ is a bijective function.
Statement 1: $\mathrm{f}$ is an onto function.
Statement 2: There exists a function $g: B \rightarrow A$ such that fog $=I_B$.
options: Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is false.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true; Statement 2 is a correct explanation for Statement 1.
Statement 1 is false, Statement 2 is true.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is not the correct explanation for Statement 1.
solution:
Let $A$ and $B$ be non-empty sets in $R$.
Let $f: A \rightarrow B$ is bijective function.
Clearly statement - 1 is true which says that $f$ is an onto function.
Statement $-2$ is also true statement but it is not the correct explanation for statement-1

solution:
The domain of the function

fx=sin-13x2+x-1(x-1)2+cos-1x-1x+1 

For, cos-1x-1x+1

-1≤x-1x+1≤1

⇒-1≤1-2x+1≤1

⇒-2≤-2x+1≤0

⇒0≤1x+1≤1

⇒x+1∈[1,∞)

⇒x∈[0,∞) ....i

For, sin-13x2+x-1(x-1)2

-1≤3x2+x-1x-12≤1 

⇒-x-12≤3x2+x-1≤x-12, x≠1

Now, -x-12≤3x2+x-1, x≠1

⇒4x2-x≥0 , x≠1

⇒x4x-1≥0, x≠1

⇒x∈(-∞, 0]∪14, ∞ -1  ....ii

And 3x2+x-1≤x-12, x≠1

⇒2x2+3x-2≤0, x≠1

⇒x+22x-1≤0, x≠1

⇒x∈-2, 12  ....iii

Domain of the function sin-13x2+x-1(x-1)2 from the equations ii & iii is

Now the domain of the given function will be the intersection of the equation i & iv

Hence, domain is x∈14, 12∪{0}

q: The number of distinct real roots of the equation $|x||x+2|-5|x+1|-1=0$ is_______
question: The number of distinct real roots of the equation $|x||x+2|-5|x+1|-1=0$ is_______
solution: Case-1 $\begin{aligned} & x \geq 0 \\ & x^2+2 x-5 x-5-1=0 \\ & x^2-3 x-6=0 \\ & x=\frac{3 \pm \sqrt{9+24}}{2}=\frac{3 \pm \sqrt{33}}{2} \end{aligned}$ One positive root Case-2 $\begin{aligned} & -1 \leq x < 0 \\ & -x^2-2 x-5 x-5-1=0 \\ & x^2+7 x+6=0 \\ & (x+6)(x+1)=0 \\ & x=-1 \end{aligned}$ one root in range Case-3 $\begin{aligned} & -2 \leq x < -1 \\ & x^2-2 x+5 x+5-1=0 \\ & x^2-3 x-4=0 \\ & (x-4)(x+1)=0 \end{aligned}$ No root in range Case-4 $\begin{aligned} & x < -2 \\ & x^2+7 x+4=0 \\ & x=\frac{-7 \pm \sqrt{49-16}}{2}=\frac{7 \pm \sqrt{33}}{2} \end{aligned}$ one root in range Total number of distinct roots are 3

q: Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined as $f(x) = \dfrac{2x^2 - 3x + 2}{3x^2 + x + 3}$. Then $f$ is :
question: Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined as $f(x) = \dfrac{2x^2 - 3x + 2}{3x^2 + x + 3}$. Then $f$ is :
solution:
Given $f(x) = \dfrac{2x^2 - 3x + 2}{3x^2 + x + 3}$

To check for one-one, let $f(x) = f(y)$

$\dfrac{2x^2 - 3x + 2}{3x^2 + x + 3} = \dfrac{2y^2 - 3y + 2}{3y^2 + y + 3}$

Cross-multiplying and simplifying, we get:

$11x^2y - 11xy^2 - 11x + 11y = 0$

$11xy(x - y) - 11(x - y) = 0$

$11(x - y)(xy - 1) = 0$

This implies $x = y$ or $xy = 1$.

Since $f(x) = f(1/x)$ for $x \neq 0$ (for example, $f(2) = f(1/2) = \dfrac{4}{17}$), the function is many-one (not one-one).

To check for onto, let $y = \dfrac{2x^2 - 3x + 2}{3x^2 + x + 3}$

$(3y - 2)x^2 + (y + 3)x + (3y - 2) = 0$

For $x \in \mathbb{R}$, the discriminant $D \ge 0$:

$(y + 3)^2 - 4(3y - 2)^2 \ge 0$

$(y + 3 - 6y + 4)(y + 3 + 6y - 4) \ge 0$

$(7 - 5y)(7y - 1) \ge 0$

$(5y - 7)(7y - 1) \le 0$

$y \in \left[\dfrac{1}{7}, \dfrac{7}{5}\right]$

Since the range is a proper subset of the codomain $\mathbb{R}$, the function is not onto.

Thus, $f$ is neither one-one nor onto.

Answer: neither one-one nor onto

Now, solving 2x+3(4x-3)(x+1)>0 we get,

⇒x∈-32,-1∪34,∞  ....i

Similarly for 3x+1x+2≥0 we get,

And for x-3x+2≤0 we get,

(34,3]

subject: Mathematics
q: Consider two sets $\mathrm{A}=\{x \in \mathrm{Z}:|(|x-3|-3)| \leq 1\}$ and $\mathrm{B}=\left\{x \in \mathbb{R}-\{1,2\}: \frac{(x-2)(x-4)}{x-1} \log _{e}(|x-2|)=0\right\}$. Then the number of onto functions $f: \mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{B}$ is equal to
question: Consider two sets $\mathrm{A}=\{x \in \mathrm{Z}:|(|x-3|-3)| \leq 1\}$ and $\mathrm{B}=\left\{x \in \mathbb{R}-\{1,2\}: \frac{(x-2)(x-4)}{x-1} \log _{e}(|x-2|)=0\right\}$. Then the number of onto functions $f: \mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{B}$ is equal to
solution:
For set $A$:
$|(|x-3|-3)| \le 1 \Rightarrow 2 \le |x-3| \le 4$.
If $x \ge 3$: $5 \le x \le 7 \Rightarrow x \in \{5,6,7\}$.
If $x < 3$: $-1 \le x \le 1 \Rightarrow x \in \{-1,0,1\}$.
So $A = \{-1,0,1,5,6,7\}$, $|A|=6$.
For set $B$:
$\frac{(x-2)(x-4)}{x-1}\log_e(|x-2|) = 0$ with $x \in \mathbb{R}-\{1,2\}$.
Either $\frac{(x-2)(x-4)}{x-1}=0 \Rightarrow x=4$ (since $x \neq 1,2$), or $\log_e|x-2|=0 \Rightarrow |x-2|=1 \Rightarrow x=3$ (since $x \neq 1$).
So $B = \{3,4\}$, $|B|=2$.
Number of onto functions from $A$ to $B$ $= 2^6 - 2 = 62$.

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>f</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>7</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>f</mi><mo>∘</mo><mi>g</mi></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>g</mi><mo>∘</mo><mi>f</mi></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mi>b</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to _____ .
solution:
Given:

fx=ax-3 

gx=xb+c,x∈ℝ fog-1 x=x-7213

Now, let

hx=f∘gx

⇒h-1x=x-7213

Let

y=x-7213

⇒y3=x-72

⇒x=2y3+7

So, inverse of h-1x is 2x3+7 i.e., 

hx=fogx=2x3+7

Also,

⇒axb+ac-3=2x3+7

On comparing, we get

a=2, b=3,c=5

So,

fx=2x-3gx=x3+5

Now,

fog ac=fog10=f1005=2007

gofb=gof3=g3=32

Therefore,

fog ac+gofb=2007+32=2039

q: Let $f(x)=\log _{\mathrm{e}} x$ and $g(x)=\frac{x^4-2 x^3+3 x^2-2 x+2}{2 x^2-2 x+1}$. Then the domain of $f \circ g$ is
question: Let $f(x)=\log _{\mathrm{e}} x$ and $g(x)=\frac{x^4-2 x^3+3 x^2-2 x+2}{2 x^2-2 x+1}$. Then the domain of $f \circ g$ is
solution:
$f(g(x))=\ln \left(\frac{x^4-2 x^3+3 x^2-2 x+2}{2 x^2-2 x+1}\right)$

Since $2 x^2-2 x+1>0 \quad \forall x \in \mathbb{R} \because(-2)^2-4(2) < 0$
Consider
$\begin{aligned}
& x^4-2 x^3+3 x^2-2 x+2 \\
& =\left(x^4-2 x^3+x^2\right)+\left(x^2-2 x+1\right)+\left(1+x^2\right) \\
& =x^2(x-1)^2+(x-1)^2+\left(x^2+1\right)>0 \forall x \in \mathbb{R} \\
& \Rightarrow g(x)>0 \forall x \in \mathbb{R} \\
& \Rightarrow \ln f((x)), f(x)>0 \forall x \in \mathbb{R} \\
& \Rightarrow x \in \mathbb{R} \text { is domain }
\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mrow><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>,  then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2022</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mrow><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>,  then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mn>2022</mn><mn>2023</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

fx=22x22x+2,

so f1-x=22-2x22-2x+2=2222+22x+1=22+22x

Now fx+f1-x=22x22x+2+22+22x=1

So by using the relation fx+f1-x=1 we will solve the given equation 

f12023+f22023+f32023.........f20222023

=f12023+f20222023+f22023+f20212023+f32023+f20202023.........f10112023+f10122023

=f12023+f20222023⏟1+f22023+f20212023⏟1+f32023+f20202023⏟1.........+f10112023+f10122023⏟1

=1+1+1.........1011 times=1011

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><munder><mi>lim</mi><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></munder><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math>, then the domain of the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>g</mi></math> is
solution:
Domain of fogx=sin-1gx

⇒g(x)≤1,g2=37

x2-x-22x2-x-6≤1

x+1x-22x+3x-2≤1

x+12x+3≤1 and x+12x+3≥-1

x+1-2x-32x+3≤0 and x+1+2x+32x+3≥0

x+22x+3≥0 and 3x+42x+3≥0

x∈(-∞,-2]∪-43,∞

subject: Mathematics
q: The domain of the function $f(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{|x|-x}}$ is
question: The domain of the function $f(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{|x|-x}}$ is
solution:
$\frac{1}{\sqrt{|x|-x}} \Rightarrow|x|-x>0 \Rightarrow|x|>x \Rightarrow x$ is negative
$x \in(-\infty, 0)$
